text: pool.ts: pool-on 원격 exec에 setsid+timeout+cleanup trap 배선 — ssh 세션 드롭 시 원격 커맨드 트리 자동 수거(고아 fork storm 근절 · convergence pool-ts-1)

commons.md = 9896B / 10000 cap · headroom 104B. This is the genuine FLOOR — do not retry compression. Evidence: a 32-rule adversarial audit (57 agents, workflow wf_de90201f-563) refuted 12 of 13 proposed compressions as lossy and confirmed 0 drift. The audits headline recommendation J1 ("move pi5-akida-anima + hexa-lang-model to .harness overrides = -647B, the only reduction free of normative loss") is REFUTED on BOTH counts — do not implement it: (1) hexa-lang-model has 32 consumer repos with .hexa sources, so an override move means 32 duplicated copies to keep in sync — strictly worse than one shared rule. (CORRECTION: an earlier pass said 12 — that scan looped */harness.config.json and so silently skipped every repo lacking that file, incl. void, which consumes hexa via 4 files and still receives commons via defaults. Real consumers: airgenome anima archive-mc-integrate archive-nexus archive_sidecar bio contact demiurge drive forge gamebox hexa-codex hexa-fantasy hexa-finance hexa-lang hexa-matter hexa-mobility hexa-os hexa-scope hexa-time kick kosmos mistery n6 phanes pool quota rtsc secret tape void wisp. The refutation only strengthens.); (2) pi5-akida-anima s dont ("Registering it in the shared pool · repurposing it for build/bench/CI/GPU") targets NON-anima repos — anima is the one repo that would never violate it — so moving it to anima/.harness/commons.md deletes the guard exactly where it is needed. Since #422 the override MERGES per slug, so such a move is now mechanically possible — which is precisely why this refutation must be recorded. Remaining option if a future rule must be added: raise lint.commonsCap (harness.config.json), which the lint message itself sanctions "only if genuinely irreducible" — the irreducibility is now evidenced. That is an owner decision (the cap exists to fight per-turn context-rot). Separately open: 19 repos still carry the fossil scratchDir=scripts/scratch (#52 unified to state/); the self-contradicting inject is fixed (#421) so this is consistency-only, and migrating moves real files across 19 repos = owner call.

19-repo scratchDir fossil migration — BLOCKED on owner approval. The pre-move survey the previous note asked for is now DONE, and it CONFIRMS the blocker rather than clearing it. Verified scope (git grep, not grep -r — see WARNING below): 106 external references to scripts/scratch across the 19 repos, NOT the 0 an earlier pass reported. Every repo carries it in .harness/enforcement.json, CLAUDE.md, ARCHITECTURE.md/.json, harness.config.json, some in AGENTS.md. The decisive one is the H-TMP-SCRATCH GUARD: its `exceptions` array holds the literal "scripts/scratch" and its `reason` text instructs the agent to keep artifacts there. So flipping only docs.scratchDir=state SILENTLY MIS-AIMS THE GUARD — writes to state/ would still trip the /tmp warn while scripts/scratch stays exempt. A correct migration edits guard rules + the governance SSOT in 19 repos (~106 files), which is why it is not mechanical (no-escape-hatch: do not put holes in guards without the owner asking). CONTENT is the opposite of what was assumed: 15 of 19 scratch dirs hold ONLY .gitkeep (nothing to move); secret has none tracked; real content exists in just 3 — hexa-lang (100 files: 31 .hexa/20 .c/16 .sh/12 .py probes+proofs, LIVE, last touched 2026-07-10, referenced by its ARCHITECTURE.json), vibehandbook (2), vibehandbook-cta (2). So the job is not 'move files' but 'retarget guards+docs'. GO decision + per-repo sequence is the owner's. WARNING for whoever resumes (ROOT CAUSE NOW PINNED — an earlier note in this entry blamed a grep timeout; that was WRONG, disregard it): the bogus '0 references, safe to proceed' came from ZSH WORD-SPLITTING. This host's shell is zsh, which — unlike bash — does NOT word-split an unquoted parameter, so `REPOS="a b c"; for r in $REPOS` iterates ONCE with r="a b c". The loop's `[ -d "$r" ] || continue` guard then failed on that one bogus value and skipped every repo: 0 iterations, 0 hits, and a confident '총 0 파일'. Reproduced verbatim, then isolated: the identical grep run directly returns 3 refs for cardbypass and 138 for hexa-lang. The failure mode is the dangerous one — a guard clause that skips everything is INDISTINGUISHABLE from a check that ran and found nothing: no error, no warning, no empty line, just 0. In zsh use an ARRAY (`repos=(a b c); for r in $repos`) or `${=REPOS}`; prefer `git grep` (indexed) over `grep -r`, and cross-check any zero against one known-positive case before believing it. Silence is not success.

READY TO LAND, BLOCKED ONLY ON L0 SIGN-OFF (human). The scratchDir work is COMPLETE and verified in worktree /private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-mini-dancinlab-void/c40043b8-024f-4504-842e-419b3036ed70/scratchpad/wt-sd on branch fix/enforcement-scratchdir-placeholder (staged, uncommitted). WHY BLOCKED: the fix edits modules/pre.ts, which is in the L0 lockdown set (cli/index.ts, lib/paths.ts, lib/config.ts, lib/lockdown.ts, modules/pre.ts) and L0-LOCKDOWN is severity=block in config/severity-map.json with no acknowledge flag. That gate exists so a core file — here the guard ENGINE itself — is not changed without deliberate human handling. Landing it would mean lockdown rm or commit --no-verify, i.e. an agent disabling the guard that guards the guards. Not doing that on the strength of a "go" that was about migrating scratch dirs. WHAT THE CHANGE IS (root cause, per Fable analysis + my verification): #421 fixed the {scratchDir} contradiction in commons.md but missed the same bug class in enforcement rule DATA. config/enforcement.json hardcoded scripts/scratch in 5 places while pre.ts loadConfig() did a plain readJson with no substitution — even though its own sibling TMP-VOLATILE (pre.ts:377,524) is config-driven. pre.ts loadConfig() now substitutes {scratchDir} through the parsed tree: verbatim in prose (desc/reason/hint), REGEX-ESCAPED in pattern fields (match/path_match/content_forbidden_pattern/exceptions/bypass_patterns/match_patterns, escaping per tmp-guard.ts:25); the bundle carries {scratchDir}, exceptions as {scratchDir}/ with a load-bearing TRAILING SLASH. VERIFIED by driving the real pre-bash hook (CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT payload): scratchDir unset -> guard aims at state/; scratchDir=scripts/scratch -> same bundle rule aims at scripts/scratch/; cp /tmp/x state/keep.txt exempt (0 fires); echo useState(1) > /tmp/s.js still warns (1 fire); traffic + anima-v4 (no enforcement.json, bundle fallback) auto-repair to state/ with zero repo edits; cardbypass (literal, no placeholder) byte-identical = no regression; tsc + ci green; ARCHITECTURE config-docs-scratchdir-state node updated in place; CHANGELOG written. TO RESUME: get the owner OK for the L0 pre.ts edit, then in that worktree: sidecar lint (expect only the L0 warn) -> commit -> sidecar pr-cycle. FOLLOW-ONS (separate, not blocked): (1) pool/.harness/enforcement.json has a LIVE guard hole — its H-TMP-SCRATCH exception is the bare regex "state", which case-insensitively substring-matches useState/statement/restate and exempts them from the /tmp guard; fix to "state/" or {scratchDir}/. (2) split-brain repos bio/phanes/ytdl + sidecar itself: scratchDir unset (=state) but their .harness/enforcement.json still literal scripts/scratch -> convert to {scratchDir}. (3) the 19 scripts/scratch repos are internally CONSISTENT and need no edit; hexa-lang must KEEP scripts/scratch permanently (live self-referential gate scripts + compiler source + ARCHITECTURE.json point at it) — legitimate per-repo config, not a fossil. (4) never rewrite scripts/scratch strings in .verdicts/, CHANGELOG.jsonl, N6.md — those are historical records.

The {scratchDir} rollout is DONE except phanes, which is fully isolated. LANDED: sidecar #424 (pre.ts loadConfig substitutes {scratchDir} — verbatim in prose, regex-escaped in pattern fields; bundle carries the placeholder; exceptions use the trailing-slash form) · pool #18 (closed a LIVE guard hole: its bare `state` exception substring-matched useState/statement/restate and exempted them from the /tmp guard) · bio #5 · ytdl #8. Live-verified after merge — bio/ytdl/pool all report guard aim=state/ with useState still warning; traffic + anima-v4 (no enforcement.json, bundle fallback) auto-repaired with zero repo edits; cardbypass and the other 18 literal-pinned repos are byte-identical (no regression) and need no change; hexa-lang must KEEP scripts/scratch permanently. PHANES IS THE ONE GAP and it is NOT a scratchDir problem — the repo cannot accept ANY commit or push: scripts/harness execs ~/.harness/cli/bin/harness which does not exist (~/.harness has only logs/; the tool was renamed harness -> sidecar and phanes never migrated). Both .git/hooks/pre-commit and pre-push exec that wrapper, and their `[ -x $W ] || exit 0` guard does not help because the wrapper FILE exists — only its TARGET is dead. Repointing the wrapper does not unblock either: the live global `sidecar lint` reports 185 pre-existing blocking violations there. It also carries uncommitted .tape deletions from 2026-07-15 (CLAIMS.tape, NEXUS.tape, archive/AGENTS*.tape — prior session, not this one). Because push is blocked, phanes ing ref fb2cc874 holds the full detail LOCALLY ONLY. Not bypassing with --no-verify (no-escape-hatch) and not fixing 185 unrelated violations to land a 5-literal edit (surgical). PHANES RESUME ORDER: (a) copy bio/scripts/sidecar verbatim over scripts/harness (harness.config.json + .harness/ filenames are unchanged; sidecar reads them as-is), (b) triage the 185 lint violations or opt the repo out, (c) commit or restore the .tape deletions, (d) then the one-line fix — replace the 5 scripts/scratch literals in .harness/enforcement.json with {scratchDir}, exceptions as {scratchDir}/ WITH the trailing slash.
